doc_description: A county-by-county USDA Rural Development Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits table effective 3-7-2025, listing eligible and ineligible jurisdictions across U.S. states and territories with varying maximum loan amounts.

structure.title: Rural Development Single Family Housing - Area Loan Limits
structure.summary: The document shows Rural Development Single Family Housing area loan limits by county, with a map-style state selector and a table of county-by-county limits. In the visible section, it focuses on Alabama and lists many Alabama counties with the same loan limit of $419,300, effective 3-7-2025.

nodes.title: ALABAMA
nodes.summary: The document is a table of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025. It lists county or equivalent jurisdictions in Alabama and Alaska with their corresponding loan limit amounts. Most Alabama counties have a uniform limit of $419,300. In Alaska, most areas also have a $419,300 limit, but several locations have higher limits such as Aleutians West, Hoonah-Angoon, Juneau, Kodiak Island, Sitka, and Skagway, while Anchorage is marked ineligible.

nodes.title: ALASKA
nodes.summary: The document is a table of Single Family Housing Direct loan limits effective 3-7-2025, listing county or equivalent areas and their corresponding maximum loan amounts. It covers Alaska and Arizona, showing that most areas have a limit of $419,300, with some locations assigned higher limits due to local housing costs. It also notes one ineligible area in Alaska (Anchorage Municipality).

nodes.title: ARIZONA
nodes.summary: The document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct loans, effective 3-7-2025, by county in Arizona and Arkansas. Most counties shown have a limit of $419,300, with a few higher limits in Arizona such as Coconino County at $450,800 and Maricopa and Pinal Counties at $437,000.

nodes.title: ARKANSAS
nodes.summary: This document is a partial table of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025. It lists county-by-county loan limit amounts for Arkansas and California. In Arkansas, the counties shown all have the same loan limit of $419,300. In California, the table shows varying county limits, with many counties at the baseline $419,300 and higher limits for certain higher-cost areas, such as $967,800 in counties like Alameda, Los Angeles, Orange, San Francisco, and Santa Clara, along with several other intermediate amounts for different counties.

nodes.title: CALIFORNIA
nodes.summary: The partial document is a table of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025 for California and Colorado counties. It lists each county or equivalent area alongside its maximum loan limit, showing that limits vary by location based on housing market conditions. Some high-cost areas have the highest limits, such as many California coastal counties at $967,800 or higher, while many lower-cost counties are set at $419,300. In Colorado, the table includes a mix of limits ranging from $419,300 to $967,800, and it also identifies certain counties or areas, such as Broomfield County and Denver County, as ineligible.

nodes.title: COLORADO
nodes.summary: The partial document lists county or equivalent area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct programs, effective 3-7-2025. It includes detailed loan limit amounts for counties in Colorado and Connecticut, showing that limits vary by county based on local area classifications. Some counties are marked “Ineligible,” while others have specific dollar limits ranging from about $419,300 to $967,800, indicating regional differences in housing loan eligibility and maximum financing amounts.

nodes.title: CONNECTICUT
nodes.summary: The partial document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct loans by county or equivalent, effective 3-7-2025. It includes Connecticut counties, showing Fairfield County with a higher limit of $680,800 and the other Connecticut counties at $419,300. It also includes Delaware counties, listing Kent County at $419,300, New Castle County at $475,600, and Sussex County at $419,300.

nodes.title: DELAWARE
nodes.summary: The partial document lists 2025 area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct programs. It shows county-level limits for Delaware—Kent County at $419,300, New Castle County at $475,600, and Sussex County at $419,300—and indicates that the District of Columbia is ineligible.

nodes.title: DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA
nodes.summary: The partial document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct programs effective 3-7-2025. It includes county-by-county loan limit amounts for the District of Columbia and Florida, showing that some counties are marked ineligible while others have specific dollar limits such as $419,300, $437,900, $464,600, and $611,800.

nodes.title: FLORIDA
nodes.summary: The partial document lists USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025 for counties in Florida and Georgia. It provides each county’s loan limit amount, with most counties shown at a standard limit of $419,300, some counties assigned higher limits such as $437,900, $451,700, $464,600, $477,400, $483,000, $523,400, $551,000, and $611,800, and some counties marked “Ineligible.” The content is formatted as a county-by-county reference table identifying local loan eligibility and maximum limits by area.

nodes.title: GEORGIA
nodes.summary: The partial document is a table of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025. It lists county or equivalent jurisdictions and their maximum loan limits, mainly for Georgia and Hawaii. Most Georgia counties have a limit of $419,300, with some counties assigned higher limits such as $444,300, $483,000, $551,000, or $600,000, $725,000, $888,700 depending on the county, while several counties are marked “Ineligible.” The Hawaii section shows higher loan limits by county, including Hawaii County, Honolulu County, Kauai County, Maui County, and Maui County - Molokai.

nodes.title: HAWAII
nodes.summary: The document lists Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025 for counties in Hawaii and Idaho. It shows the maximum loan amount by county or equivalent area, including Hawaii County, Honolulu County, Kauai County, Maui County, and Maui County - Molokai, followed by a long county-by-county table for Idaho with varying limits such as $419,300, $498,500, $515,400, $517,000, $607,200, and others.

nodes.title: IDAHO
nodes.summary: The partial document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct programs, effective 3-7-2025, organized by county for Idaho and Illinois. It shows the maximum loan limit amount for each county or marks some counties as ineligible. Most counties share a standard limit of $419,300, while some counties have higher limits such as Ada County ($498,500), Kootenai County ($515,400), Lincoln County ($517,000), Blaine County and Camas County ($607,200), and Teton County ($967,800). In Illinois, the excerpt begins an alphabetical county list and includes examples of ineligible counties like Cook County and DuPage County.

nodes.title: ILLINOIS
nodes.summary: The document is a state-by-state loan limit table for USDA Single Family Housing Direct programs, effective 3-7-2025. It lists counties or equivalent areas in Illinois and Indiana and assigns each an area loan limit, mostly $419,300. Some counties in Illinois, such as Cook, DuPage, and Lake, are marked ineligible rather than given a dollar limit. The main focus is on geographic eligibility and the maximum loan amount available by county.

nodes.title: INDIANA
nodes.summary: The document is a table of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025 for Indiana and Iowa. It lists counties or equivalent jurisdictions and their corresponding loan limit amounts, which are generally $419,300 across the counties shown. One Indiana county, Marion County, is marked ineligible.

nodes.title: IOWA
nodes.summary: The document is a table of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025, listing counties by state and the maximum loan limit for each. It covers Iowa and Kansas counties shown in the excerpt, and every county listed has the same limit of $419,300.

nodes.title: KANSAS
nodes.summary: The partial document is a table of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025. It lists counties in Kansas and Kentucky and shows the loan limit for each county, which is generally $419,300. The document also includes repeated section headers and indicates one exception in Kentucky: Fayette County is marked ineligible rather than assigned a loan limit.

nodes.title: KENTUCKY
nodes.summary: The partial document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct loans, effective 3-7-2025, organized by county or parish for Kentucky and Louisiana. Most listed counties/parishes have a loan limit of $419,300, while a few locations are marked ineligible, including Fayette County and Jefferson County in Kentucky, and Orleans Parish in Louisiana.

nodes.title: LOUISIANA
nodes.summary: The document lists USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025 for counties/parishes in Louisiana and Maine. Most locations shown have a limit of $419,300, with a few higher-limit exceptions in Maine at $480,200. It also marks Orleans Parish as ineligible.

nodes.title: MAINE
nodes.summary: The document lists county-by-county single-family housing direct loan limits effective March 7, 2025, for Maine and Maryland. It shows that most counties in Maine have a limit of $419,300, with Cumberland, Sagadahoc, and York counties at $480,200. In Maryland, limits vary more widely: many counties are set at $419,300, some at $475,600 or $585,100, and several higher-cost counties such as Calvert, Charles, Frederick, Montgomery, and Prince George’s are at $967,800. Baltimore city is marked ineligible.

nodes.title: MARYLAND
nodes.summary: The document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct loans, effective 3-7-2025, for counties in Maryland and Massachusetts. It shows the maximum loan limit amounts by county or marks some areas as ineligible. Maryland counties are grouped mostly into limits of $419,300, $585,100, or $967,800, with Baltimore city marked ineligible. Massachusetts counties are listed with limits such as $419,300, $588,800, $598,000, $731,400, and $967,800, with Suffolk County marked ineligible.

nodes.title: MASSACHUSETTS
nodes.summary: The document is a table of area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct loans, effective 3-7-2025, listing county-by-county limits for Massachusetts and Michigan. It shows different maximum loan amounts by county, with most Massachusetts counties assigned limits such as $419,300, $588,800, $731,400, or $967,800, while Suffolk County is marked ineligible. The Michigan section continues with a long list of counties, and the counties shown there are generally set at a $419,300 limit.

nodes.title: MICHIGAN
nodes.summary: The document is a list of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective March 7, 2025. It shows county-by-county loan limit amounts for Michigan and Minnesota. Most Michigan counties are listed with the same limit of $419,300. In Minnesota, most counties also have a limit of $419,300, while a few counties have a higher limit of $423,200. The document also identifies some counties as “ineligible,” meaning they do not qualify for the listed loan program limits.

nodes.title: MINNESOTA
nodes.summary: The document is a table of U.S. county-level area loan limits for the USDA Single Family Housing Direct program, effective 3-7-2025. It lists counties in Minnesota and Mississippi and shows the maximum loan limit for each county, with most counties set at $419,300, some Minnesota counties at a higher $423,200 limit, and certain counties marked as ineligible.

nodes.title: MISSISSIPPI
nodes.summary: The document is a list of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025 for counties in Mississippi and Missouri. It shows county-by-county borrowing limits, with the visible counties in both states all set at $419,300. The excerpt appears to be part of a larger state-by-state table of loan limits for eligible rural areas.

nodes.title: MISSOURI
nodes.summary: The partial document is a list of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025, organized by county or equivalent jurisdiction. It shows that most listed counties in Missouri and Montana have a loan limit of $419,300, while a few counties in Montana have higher limits such as Carbon County ($603,500), Flathead County ($458,100), Gallatin County ($575,000), Missoula County ($448,000), and Park County ($450,800). It also identifies certain Missouri areas, specifically St. Louis city and St. Louis County, as ineligible.

nodes.title: MONTANA
nodes.summary: The document lists county-by-county area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct loans, effective 3-7-2025, for Montana and Nebraska. It provides specific dollar limits for each county or equivalent area, showing that most counties share a standard limit of $419,300 while certain higher-cost counties have elevated limits such as $448,000, $450,800, $458,100, $483,000, $575,000, and $603,500. The main purpose of the document is to define geographic loan eligibility limits by county for these two states.

nodes.title: NEBRASKA
nodes.summary: The partial document lists USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025 for counties in Nebraska and Nevada. Most Nebraska counties have a limit of $419,300, with a few higher-limit counties such as Dawson County and Gosper County at $483,000. The Nevada section shows most counties at $419,300, with higher limits for Douglas County at $575,000 and Storey and Washoe Counties at $506,000, and Carson City marked ineligible.

nodes.title: NEVADA
nodes.summary: The document lists county-level loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct programs effective 3-7-2025. It provides Nevada and New Hampshire area loan limits, showing most counties at $419,300, with some higher limits such as Douglas County in Nevada at $575,000, Storey and Washoe Counties at $506,000, and certain New Hampshire counties like Rockingham and Strafford at $731,400. It also notes that Carson City, Nevada is ineligible.

nodes.title: NEW HAMPSHIRE
nodes.summary: The document lists county-level area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct programs, effective 3-7-2025, for New Hampshire and New Jersey. It shows each county or equivalent area with its corresponding loan limit amount, and marks some counties as ineligible. In New Hampshire, most counties have limits of $419,300, with higher limits for Hillsborough, Rockingham, and Strafford counties. In New Jersey, the limits vary widely by county, ranging from $419,300 to $967,800, with several counties listed as ineligible.

nodes.title: NEW JERSEY
nodes.summary: The partial document lists county-by-county area loan limits for the USDA Single Family Housing Direct program, effective 3-7-2025, for New Jersey and New Mexico. It shows the maximum loan limit for each county, with some counties marked ineligible in New Jersey and varying limits such as $419,300, $475,600, $533,600, and $967,800. In New Mexico, most counties have a limit of $419,300, with a few exceptions like Los Alamos County at $553,800 and Santa Fe County at $433,300.

nodes.title: NEW MEXICO
nodes.summary: The partial document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct programs, effective 3-7-2025, for counties in New Mexico and New York. It provides the maximum loan limit for each county or notes when a county is ineligible. Most listed counties have a limit of $419,300, while some have higher limits such as Los Alamos County ($553,800), Santa Fe County ($433,300), Dutchess County ($581,200), and Orange County ($581,200). Several New York counties, including Bronx, Kings, Nassau, and New York County, are marked ineligible.

nodes.title: NEW YORK
nodes.summary: The partial document is a table of Area Loan Limits for Single Family Housing Direct loans, effective 3-7-2025, listing county-by-county loan limit amounts. It shows New York counties first, including many counties with a standard limit of $419,300, some higher-limit counties such as Dutchess, Orange, Putnam, Rockland, Suffolk, and Westchester, and several counties marked ineligible. The document then continues with North Carolina counties, again listing each county and its corresponding loan limit, with most at $419,300 and a few counties assigned higher limits such as Camden, Chatham, Currituck, Dare, Durham, Franklin, Gates, and Granville.

nodes.title: NORTH CAROLINA
nodes.summary: The document is a table of area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct, effective 3-7-2025. It lists counties or county equivalents in North Carolina and North Dakota along with their corresponding loan limit amounts. Most counties have a standard limit of $419,300, while some counties have higher limits such as $424,100, $506,000, $530,800, $606,200, and $644,000. The document is organized by state and county, showing regional variations in loan eligibility limits.

nodes.title: NORTH DAKOTA
nodes.summary: The document lists county-level area loan limits for the USDA Single Family Housing Direct program, effective March 7, 2025. It includes entries for North Dakota and Ohio, showing the maximum loan limit for each county or marking a county as ineligible. Most counties are assigned a limit of $419,300, while a few Ohio counties have a higher limit of $455,400. The main purpose is to provide an updated reference of geographic loan eligibility and loan cap amounts by county.

nodes.title: OHIO
nodes.summary: The document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct loans, effective 3-7-2025, by county or equivalent. It includes county-by-county limits for Ohio and Oklahoma, showing most counties with a limit of $419,300, some higher-cost counties in Ohio with a limit of $455,400, and a few counties marked ineligible.

nodes.title: OKLAHOMA
nodes.summary: The document lists USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025 for counties in Oklahoma and Oregon. It shows that most Oklahoma counties have a uniform loan limit of $419,300. In Oregon, the limits vary by county, with many counties at $419,300 but higher limits in some areas such as Benton, Clackamas, Columbia, Multnomah, Washington, and Yamhill counties, as well as especially high limits in Deschutes County and Hood River County.

nodes.title: OREGON
nodes.summary: The partial document lists Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025 for counties in Oregon and Pennsylvania. It provides county-by-county maximum loan amounts, showing most counties at a standard limit of $419,300, with higher limits in certain higher-cost areas such as Clackamas, Columbia, Multnomah, Washington, and Yamhill counties in Oregon, and Bucks, Chester, and Delaware counties in Pennsylvania.

nodes.title: PENNSYLVANIA
nodes.summary: The document lists area loan limits for the USDA Single Family Housing Direct program, effective 3-7-2025, for counties in Pennsylvania and municipios in Puerto Rico. It shows the maximum loan limit assigned to each location, with most Pennsylvania counties set at $419,300, several higher-limit counties such as Bucks, Chester, Delaware, and Montgomery at $475,600, Pike County at $967,800, and Philadelphia County marked ineligible. For Puerto Rico, many municipios are listed with limits of $419,300 or $552,000, with some exceptions such as Culebra at $425,900 and Cataño marked ineligible.

nodes.title: PUERTO RICO
nodes.summary: The document lists Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025 for counties or county-equivalent areas. It shows a table of municipalities in Puerto Rico with their corresponding loan limits, which are mostly either $419,300 or $552,000, with some areas marked ineligible and one at $425,900. It then lists Rhode Island counties and their limits, showing Bristol County as ineligible and the other counties—Kent, Newport, Providence, and Washington—each with a loan limit of $588,800.

nodes.title: RHODE ISLAND
nodes.summary: The document lists USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025 for counties in Rhode Island and South Carolina. It shows that Bristol County, Rhode Island is ineligible, while other Rhode Island counties have a limit of $588,800. For South Carolina, most counties have a loan limit of $419,300, with higher limits in some counties such as Beaufort and Jasper at $487,600, and Berkeley, Charleston, and Dorchester at $506,000.

nodes.title: SOUTH CAROLINA
nodes.summary: The document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct programs effective 3-7-2025. It provides county-by-county loan limit amounts for South Carolina and South Dakota, showing that most counties have a standard limit of $419,300, with some higher limits in select counties such as Beaufort, Jasper, Berkeley, Charleston, and Dorchester in South Carolina, and Lawrence County in South Dakota.

nodes.title: SOUTH DAKOTA
nodes.summary: The document is a table of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025, listing county-by-county loan cap amounts for South Dakota and Tennessee. Most counties shown have a limit of $419,300, with a few higher limits highlighted, such as Lawrence County in South Dakota at $433,300 and several Tennessee counties at $791,200, plus Hickman County at $427,800.

nodes.title: TENNESSEE
nodes.summary: The document is a table of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025. It lists counties or equivalents in Tennessee and Texas along with their corresponding loan limit amounts. Most counties have a standard limit of $419,300, while some counties have higher limits such as $427,800, $446,200, $450,800, $457,200, $712,000, and $791,200, indicating different geographic loan eligibility tiers by county.

nodes.title: TEXAS
nodes.summary: The document is a county-by-county list of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025. It shows Texas and Utah counties, along with the maximum loan limit assigned to each county, with most Texas counties at $419,300 and some higher limits such as $446,200, $450,800, and $457,200. A few Texas counties are highlighted with even higher or distinct amounts, and some Utah counties have much larger limits such as $595,200, $797,600, and $931,000. The document also marks certain counties as “ineligible,” indicating no loan eligibility in those areas.

nodes.title: UTAH
nodes.summary:
The partial document lists county-by-county area loan limits for the USDA Single Family Housing Direct program, effective 3-7-2025, for two states: Utah and Vermont. It shows each county or equivalent jurisdiction with a corresponding loan limit amount, and marks some counties as “ineligible” rather than assigning a dollar limit.

For Utah, most counties have limits of $419,300, with higher limits in certain counties such as Box Elder and Cache ($595,200), Grand ($567,600), Juab and Utah ($481,100), Rich ($463,600), Tooele ($503,200), Washington ($534,000), Wayne ($797,600), and Summit and Wasatch ($931,000). Davis, Salt Lake, and Weber Counties are listed as ineligible.

For Vermont, the document lists all counties and their loan limits, mostly $419,300, with higher limits for Chittenden ($460,000), Franklin ($460,000), and Grand Isle ($460,000).

nodes.title: VERMONT
nodes.summary: The partial document lists county or equivalent area loan limits effective 3-7-2025 for Vermont and the Virgin Islands under Single Family Housing Direct loan limits. It shows that most Vermont counties have a limit of $419,300, while Chittenden, Franklin, and Grand Isle Counties have a higher limit of $460,000. For the Virgin Islands, the limits are $419,300 for St. Croix, $891,400 for St. John, and $538,200 for St. Thomas.

nodes.title: VIRGIN ISLANDS
nodes.summary: The document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct programs effective 3-7-2025, organized by county or equivalent jurisdiction. It includes specific loan limit amounts for locations in the Virgin Islands and Virginia, and marks some cities and counties as ineligible. The excerpt shows varying limit tiers, such as $419,300, $473,800, $538,200, $539,100, and higher limits like $891,400 and $967,800 for certain areas.

nodes.title: VIRGINIA
nodes.summary: This document lists county or equivalent area loan limits for the USDA Single Family Housing Direct program, effective 3-7-2025, for Virginia and Washington. It identifies each county or city as either eligible with a specific loan limit amount or ineligible, showing different limit tiers across areas. In Virginia, many counties and a few cities have limits ranging from $419,300 up to $967,800, with numerous cities marked ineligible. In Washington, the listed counties have loan limits mostly at $419,300, with some higher limits such as $437,000, $446,200, $450,800, $460,000, $463,600, $483,000, $506,000, $556,600, $829,800, and several areas included by county.

nodes.title: WASHINGTON
nodes.summary: The document is a table of area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct loans effective 3-7-2025. It lists county-by-county maximum loan limit amounts for Washington and West Virginia. Most counties have a standard limit of $419,300, while certain higher-cost counties have elevated limits such as King, Pierce, and Snohomish counties in Washington at $829,800, and Jefferson County in West Virginia at $967,800. The section is organized by state and county or equivalent jurisdiction, showing how loan limits vary by location.

nodes.title: WEST VIRGINIA
nodes.summary: The partial document lists Area Loan Limits for USDA Single Family Housing Direct loans, effective 3-7-2025, by county in two states: West Virginia and Wisconsin. Most counties shown have a limit of $419,300. In West Virginia, Jefferson County is highlighted with a higher limit of $967,800, while in Wisconsin, Milwaukee County is marked ineligible. The excerpt is organized as a county-by-county table showing the applicable loan limit or eligibility status.

nodes.title: WISCONSIN
nodes.summary: The partial document is a table of USDA Single Family Housing Direct area loan limits effective 3-7-2025. It lists county-by-county loan limit amounts for Wisconsin and Wyoming, showing that most counties have a limit of $419,300, with a few exceptions such as Pierce County and Sauk County at $423,200, Sublette County at $472,000, and Teton County at $967,800. It also marks Milwaukee County as ineligible.

nodes.title: WYOMING
nodes.summary: The document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct loans effective 3-7-2025. It provides county-by-county limits for Wyoming, showing most counties at $419,300, with higher limits for Sublette County ($472,000) and Teton County ($967,800). It also includes limits for U.S. territories: American Samoa ($419,300), Guam ($450,800), and the Northern Mariana Islands ($423,200).

nodes.title: AMERICAN SAMOA
nodes.summary: The partial document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct loans, showing the county-equivalent limit effective March 7, 2025 for U.S. territories. It includes American Samoa at $419,300, Guam at $450,800, and the Northern Mariana Islands at $423,200.

nodes.title: GUAM
nodes.summary: The partial document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct loans, effective 3-7-2025, for U.S. territories. It shows the county or equivalent limit amounts for American Samoa ($419,300), Guam ($450,800), and the Northern Mariana Islands ($423,200).

nodes.title: NORTHERN MARIANA ISLANDS
nodes.summary: The partial document lists area loan limits for Single Family Housing Direct loans, showing the county or equivalent effective limits as of 3-7-2025 for U.S. territories. It includes American Samoa at $419,300, Guam at $450,800, and the Northern Mariana Islands at $423,200.
